data.description: Allows a character to work and earn money between adventures, maintaining a modest lifestyle that allows them to pay for their daily expenses (see Chapter 5 for information on lifestyle expenses). This benefit lasts until the next adventure or until the character decides to change their profession. The section describes how characters can practice a profession, including employment options such as being a member of an organization like a temple, thieves' guild, or other gainful employment that provides pay and covers the cost of maintaining a comfortable lifestyle.

You must spend gold piece (gp) equal to at least half the market value of the item you want to craft. You can make an item worth less than its full market value by adding value to your crafting process in increments of 5 gp per day until you reach the market value. For example, a suit of plate armor valued at 1,500 gp (market value) takes 300 days to craft yourself.

### TRAINING
Multiple characters can combine their efforts toward crafting a single item. To do this, each character must have training in the relevant skill and access to working facilities, such as tools or workshops. Characters can work together at the same place, with each contributing gp worth of effort per day equivalent to 5 gp worth of daily effort spent on helping craft the item. For example, three characters can craft a suit of plate armor in 100 days at a total cost of 750 gp.

While maintaining a modest or comfortable lifestyle for crafting, you can forego having to pay 1 gp per day.

to can downtime between adventures recover from debilitating injury, or disease, poison. After days downtime recuperating, spent three of you make DC Constitution saving throw. can a 15 On successful you choose of following
you prevents you from regaining hit points. For the next 24 hours, advantage on saving throws against disease and poison affecting you.

During the downtime activity known as research, characters can gain insight into mysteries that unfurled during the course of the campaign. This includes poring over dusty tomes and crumbling scrolls in a library or buying rumors and gossip from adventuring locales. The DM determines whether the information is available and how many days it will take to find the necessary tools and materials. On any given day, you must spend at least 1 gp to cover expenses, which are in addition to your normal lifestyle expenses discussed in chapter 5.

a character can spend a set of tools with an instructor to learn a new language. The training lasts 250 days and costs 1 gp per day. After completing the requisite amount of time, the character gains proficiency in the new language.
